input: @kody we prefer using the :has() selector instead of JavaScript for these CSS cases.

expected.rule: prefer CSS :has() over JavaScript for these CSS cases

input: @kody the `core` domain is special and should always include infrastructure classes, even if the domain name is missing.

expected.rule: core domain should always include infrastructure classes even if domain name is missing

input: @kody we haven't stabilized our API yet, so let it fail fast and loud instead of using defensive guards.

expected.rule: allow fail fast for unstable API instead of defensive guards

input: @kody in this project, we never use an ORM for domain entities.

expected.rule: never use ORM for domain entities in this project

input: @kody this is a game system, so the 'private key' represents in-game access, not actual financial assets.

expected.rule: in this game system private key means in-game access, not financial assets

input: @kody our architecture requires Kodak to publish a message to the SNS topic first, so we use feature flags to handle the transition.

expected.rule: publish message to SNS topic first as per architecture; use feature flags for transition

input: @kody the duplicate mutation is by design for API flexibility, both share the same rate limiting implementation.

expected.rule: duplicate mutation is intentional for API flexibility and shares same rate limiting

input: @kody we use UUIDs for all our database IDs, so a strict string comparison is completely safe here.

expected.rule: use UUIDs for all database IDs

input: @kody we always use dependency injection instead of global mocks in our test files.

expected.rule: always use dependency injection instead of global mocks in tests

input: @kody we use a shared parameter group for all production clusters by design.

expected.rule: use shared parameter group for all production clusters by design

input: @kody o projeto não utiliza ORM e por regra de negócio as entidades não devem ter propriedades nulas por padrão.

expected.rule: o projeto não utiliza ORM e entidades não devem ter propriedades nulas por padrão

input: @kody no nosso sistema, grupos do WhatsApp possuem um ID específico gerado por eles, então eles sempre são tratados como numbers.

expected.rule: grupos do WhatsApp possuem ID específico e são tratados como numbers

input: @kody a API da SEFAZ Bahia funciona de forma síncrona agora, então não precisamos tratar de forma assíncrona.

expected.rule: API da SEFAZ Bahia é síncrona, não precisa de tratamento assíncrono

input: @kody a gente sempre usa o helper sanitize junto com o j para evitar XSS nas views do Rails.

expected.rule: sempre usar helper sanitize com j para evitar XSS nas views Rails

input: @kody a tabela service_access_management já possui index, então a gente prefere usar NOT EXISTS ao invés de LEFT JOIN por questão de legibilidade.

expected.rule: preferir NOT EXISTS em vez de LEFT JOIN para service_access_management

input: @kody nós usamos a classe CodeManagementInterface como abstração para todos os serviços de git, então precisamos manter esse padrão de implementação.

expected.rule: usar CodeManagementInterface como abstração para serviços de git

input: @kody o endpoint não é público, ele funciona apenas para clientes do usuário logado, então não há risco de expor dados abertamente.

expected.rule: private endpoint only for logged-in user's clients, no risk of open data exposure

input: @kody nós garantimos a integridade via banco, então todo produto cadastrado sempre terá um preço associado, o INNER JOIN é intencional.

expected.rule: garantimos integridade via banco, todo produto tem preço, usar INNER JOIN é intencional

input: @kody a duplicação aqui é intencional para evitar problemas de contexto com o before_action que poderia gerar um DoubleRenderError.

expected.rule: intentional duplication to avoid DoubleRenderError with before_action context

input: @kody fila com mode sequential não utiliza o prefetch na nossa configuração, apenas as de mode concurrent.

expected.rule: fila mode sequential não usa prefetch; apenas mode concurrent usa

input: @kody los criterios se usan solo para validar el IF si le pego a un endpoint o al otro, no para pasarlos como parámetro.

expected.rule: Criteria is used only for IF validation to determine which endpoint is hit, not as a parameter to be passed.

input: @kody si la variable de entorno no existe debe fallar en el arranque, no queremos que la app levante con un valor inválido o por defecto.

expected.rule: falhar no arranque se variável de ambiente não existir

input: @kody esto se resuelve en la configuración, nunca asignamos una cadena vacía si no hay variable de entorno.

expected.rule: nunca atribuir string vazia quando variável de ambiente não existe

input: @kody please remember that we never commit `.env` files to the repository.

expected.rule: never commit .env files to the repository

input: @kody make a note: always use `React.FC` when defining functional components in this project.

expected.rule: always use React.FC when defining functional components

input: @kody save this to your memory: our primary database is PostgreSQL, not MySQL.

expected.rule: primary database is PostgreSQL, not MySQL

input: @kody remember, we don't use the default export in TypeScript files, only named exports.

expected.rule: use named exports only in TypeScript files

input: @kody memorize this rule: all new components must have a corresponding `.test.tsx` file.

expected.rule: all new components must have a corresponding .test.tsx file

input: @kody from now on, remember that we use `styled-components` for styling, not CSS modules.

expected.rule: use styled-components instead of CSS modules

input: @kody add this to your memory: the maximum line length for our linter is 120 characters.

expected.rule: maximum linter line length is 120 characters

input: @kody please remember to never suggest `eval()` under any circumstances.

expected.rule: never suggest eval()

input: @kody store this preference: we enforce strict null checks in all our TypeScript configurations.

expected.rule: enforce strict null checks in TypeScript configuration

input: @kody lembre-se que neste projeto nós sempre utilizamos `async/await` ao invés de `.then()`.

expected.rule: usar async/await ao invés de .then()

input: @kody grave essa informação: todas as rotas da API devem ser prefixadas com `/api/v1`.

expected.rule: API routes must be prefixed with /api/v1

input: @kody anota aí para o futuro: não usamos Redux neste projeto, o gerenciamento de estado global é feito com Zustand.

expected.rule: do not use Redux; use Zustand for global state

input: @kody por favor, lembre-se de nunca sugerir alterações no arquivo `package-lock.json` manualmente.

expected.rule: never suggest manual edits to package-lock.json

input: @kody guarde na memória: o deploy de produção acontece automaticamente ao fazer merge na branch `main`.

expected.rule: production deploy happens automatically on merge to main branch

input: @kody memorize esta regra: variáveis de ambiente expostas no frontend devem sempre começar com `NEXT_PUBLIC_`.

expected.rule: frontend-exposed environment variables must start with NEXT_PUBLIC_

input: @kody recuerda que siempre debemos manejar las excepciones globales en el componente `ErrorBoundary`.

expected.rule: global exceptions should be handled by ErrorBoundary

input: @kody guarda esto en tu memoria: la autenticación se realiza siempre mediante tokens JWT enviados en los headers.

expected.rule: authentication is done via JWT tokens in headers

input: @kody haz una nota mental: no usamos la librería Lodash aquí, preferimos usar los métodos nativos de los arrays de JavaScript.

expected.rule: prefer native JavaScript array methods over Lodash

input: @kody recuerda, para manipular fechas siempre utilizamos la librería `date-fns` en lugar de `moment.js`.

expected.rule: use date-fns instead of moment.js for date handling

input: @kody por favor recuerda que los nombres de las tablas en nuestra base de datos siempre van en plural y snake_case.

expected.rule: database table names should be plural and snake_case

input: @kody do you remember what we decided about the database schema yesterday?
@kody I prefer to just hardcode it for this specific test so we can move on.

expected.rule: allow hardcoding values in tests for expediency when appropriate

input: @kody I can't remember the exact syntax for the `reduce` function.
@kody remember to close the database connection after you run this specific script.

expected.rule: close database connection after running this specific script

input: @kody what are the coding standards we usually prefer?
@kody we prefer doing the deployment on Fridays, right?
@kody for this specific component, we don't use the standard padding.

expected.rule: this specific component does not use standard padding

input: @kody we generally use AWS for everything, but for this tiny project let's just use Vercel.

expected.rule: prefer AWS for everything except this tiny project where we use Vercel

@kody apenas dessa vez, eu prefiro que você me explique passo a passo em vez de dar o código.

@kody guarda este archivo temporalmente mientras revisamos el otro controlador.

@kody list active Kody Rules at the organization level.
@kody create a Kody issue for this bug in src/auth/token.ts with severity high.

@kody memorize this temporary hotfix until we deploy tomorrow morning.

@kody remember to rerun this script once after lunch and then forget it.
@kody, in this microservice, we strictly follow the 'Hexagonal Architecture'. Business logic must stay in the `domain` layer and have zero dependencies on the `infrastructure` layer.

expected.rule: Follow Hexagonal Architecture: domain layer must have zero dependencies on infrastructure layer.

input: @kody, for the payment processing module, never use floating-point numbers for currency. Always use the `Money` value object or integers representing cents.

expected.rule: Never use floats for currency; use Money value object or integer cents.

input: @kody, we are currently migrating from AWS SDK v2 to v3. If you see v2 imports in new PRs, flag them as blockers.

expected.rule: Block AWS SDK v2 imports; require migration to v3 for new code.

input: @kody, don't worry about the lack of unit tests for the `LegacyConnector` class; it's scheduled for deletion next month.

expected.rule: Exclude LegacyConnector class from unit test requirements as it is deprecated.

input: @kody, when reviewing SQL, prioritize `EXISTS` over `COUNT(*)` for existence checks. Our DBA found it performs significantly better on our Postgres clusters.

expected.rule: Prefer EXISTS over COUNT(*) for existence checks for Postgres performance.

input: @kody, for this specific refactor, please ignore the cyclomatic complexity warnings. I'll fix them in a separate ticket.
explanation: Transient instruction for a specific PR/moment, not a long-term rule.

input: @kody, memorize this: any function that interacts with the `UserStore` must be wrapped in our internal `withAuditLog` HOC for SOC2 compliance.

expected.rule: Wrap UserStore interactions in withAuditLog HOC for SOC2 compliance.

input: @kody, we use a custom 'Internal-' prefix for all our custom headers. Never use standard 'X-' prefixes.

expected.rule: Use 'Internal-' prefix for custom headers instead of 'X-'.

input: @kody, for the next 2 hours, please focus only on finding potential race conditions in this PR.
@kody, note that we favor composition over inheritance in our React components. If you see deep inheritance chains, suggest a refactor using hooks.

expected.rule: Favor composition over inheritance; suggest hooks to replace deep inheritance.

input: @kody, please remember that we use `camelCase` for JSON keys in the API, but `snake_case` for database columns.

expected.rule: Use camelCase for API JSON keys and snake_case for database columns.
